args.name: v8_android_log_stdout
args.comment: Print to stdout on Android.

args.name: v8_custom_deps
args.comment: Dynamically set an additional dependency from v8/custom_deps.

args.name: v8_enable_future
args.comment: Sets -DV8_ENABLE_FUTURE.

args.name: v8_enable_system_instrumentation
args.comment: Sets -DENABLE_SYSTEM_INSTRUMENTATION. Enables OS-dependent event tracing

args.name: v8_etw_guid
args.comment: Sets the GUID for the ETW provider

args.name: v8_enable_verify_heap
args.comment: Sets -DVERIFY_HEAP.

args.name: v8_enable_verify_write_barriers
args.comment: Sets -DVERIFY_WRITE_BARRIERS.

args.name: v8_enable_verify_predictable
args.comment: Sets -DVERIFY_PREDICTABLE

args.name: v8_deprecation_warnings
args.comment: Enable compiler warnings when using V8_DEPRECATED apis.

args.name: v8_imminent_deprecation_warnings
args.comment: Enable compiler warnings when using V8_DEPRECATE_SOON apis.

args.name: v8_embed_script
args.comment: Embeds the given script into the snapshot.

args.name: v8_embedder_string
args.comment: Allows the embedder to add a custom suffix to the version string.

args.name: v8_enable_disassembler
args.comment: Sets -DENABLE_DISASSEMBLER.

args.name: v8_enable_regexp_diagnostics
args.comment: Sets -DV8_ENABLE_REGEXP_DIAGNOSTICS.

args.name: v8_promise_internal_field_count
args.comment: Sets the number of internal fields on promise objects.

args.name: v8_array_buffer_internal_field_count
args.comment: Sets the number of internal fields on array buffer objects.

args.name: v8_array_buffer_view_internal_field_count
args.comment: Sets the number of internal fields on array buffer view objects.

args.name: v8_enable_vtunejit
args.comment: Sets -DENABLE_VTUNE_JIT_INTERFACE.

args.name: v8_enable_vtunetracemark
args.comment: Sets -DENABLE_VTUNE_TRACEMARK.

args.name: v8_enable_apx_f
args.comment: Sets -DV8_ENABLE_APX_F.

args.name: v8_enable_hugepage
args.comment: Sets -DENABLE_HUGEPAGE

args.name: v8_enable_private_mapping_fork_optimization
args.comment: Sets -DV8_ENABLE_PRIVATE_MAPPING_FORK_OPTIMIZATION.  This flag speeds up the performance of fork/execve on Linux systems for embedders which use it (like Node.js). It works by marking the pages that V8 allocates as MADV_DONTFORK. Without MADV_DONTFORK, the Linux kernel spends a long time manipulating page mappings on fork and exec which the child process doesn't generally need to access.  See v8:7381 for more details.

args.name: v8_enable_handle_zapping
args.comment: Sets the default for v8_enable_local_handle_zapping and v8_enable_global_handle_zapping.

args.name: v8_enable_local_handle_zapping
args.comment: Sets -DENABLE_LOCAL_HANDLE_ZAPPING, which is more expensive than just the global handles.  By default it is enabled if v8_enable_handle_zapping is enabled.

args.name: v8_enable_global_handle_zapping
args.comment: Sets -DENABLE_GLOBAL_HANDLE_ZAPPING.  By default it is enabled if v8_enable_handle_zapping is enabled.

args.name: v8_enable_slow_dchecks
args.comment: Enable slow dchecks.

args.name: v8_enable_fast_mksnapshot
args.comment: Enable fast mksnapshot runs.

args.name: v8_enable_concurrent_mksnapshot
args.comment: Enable using multiple threads to build builtins in mksnapshot.

args.name: v8_enable_fast_torque
args.comment: Optimize code for Torque executable, even during a debug build.

args.name: v8_win64_unwinding_info
args.comment: Enable the registration of unwinding info for Windows x64 and ARM64.

args.name: v8_enable_snapshot_code_comments
args.comment: Enable code comments for builtins in the snapshot (impacts performance). This also enables v8_code_comments.

args.name: v8_code_comments
args.comment: Allow runtime-enabled code comments (with --code-comments). Enabled by default in debug builds. Sets -DV8_CODE_COMMENTS

args.name: v8_enable_debug_code
args.comment: Allow runtime-enabled debug code (with --debug-code). Enabled by default in debug builds. Sets -DV8_ENABLE_DEBUG_CODE

args.name: v8_enable_snapshot_native_code_counters
args.comment: Enable native counters from the snapshot (impacts performance, sets -DV8_SNAPSHOT_NATIVE_CODE_COUNTERS). This option will generate extra code in the snapshot to increment counters, as per the --native-code-counters flag.

args.name: v8_enable_static_roots
args.comment: Use pre-generated static root pointer values from static-roots.h.

args.name: v8_enable_static_roots_generation
args.comment: Mode used by gen-static-roots.py to have a heap layout which is identical to when v8_enable_static_roots is enabled.

args.name: v8_enable_verify_csa
args.comment: Enable code-generation-time checking of types in the CodeStubAssembler.

args.name: v8_enable_object_print
args.comment: Sets -DOBJECT_PRINT.

args.name: v8_always_enable_maglev_graph_labeller
args.comment: Sets -DALWAYS_MAGLEV_GRAPH_LABELLER.

args.name: v8_enable_trace_maps
args.comment: Sets -DV8_TRACE_MAPS.

args.name: v8_enable_v8_checks
args.comment: Sets -DV8_ENABLE_CHECKS.

args.name: v8_enable_memory_accounting_checks
args.comment: Sets -DV8_ENABLE_MEMORY_ACCOUNTING_CHECKS

args.name: v8_enable_trace_unoptimized
args.comment: Sets -DV8_TRACE_UNOPTIMIZED.

args.name: v8_enable_trace_feedback_updates
args.comment: Sets -DV8_TRACE_FEEDBACK_UPDATES.

args.name: v8_enable_atomic_object_field_writes
args.comment: Sets -DV8_ATOMIC_OBJECT_FIELD_WRITES and turns all field write operations into relaxed atomic operations.

args.name: v8_enable_concurrent_marking
args.comment: Controls the default value of v8_enable_concurrent_marking_state. See the default setting code below.

args.name: v8_enable_ignition_dispatch_counting
args.comment: Sets -DV8_IGNITION_DISPATCH_COUNTING. Enables counting frequencies of bytecode dispatches. After building in this configuration, subsequent runs of d8 can output frequencies for each pair of (current, next) bytecode instructions executed if you specify --trace-ignition-dispatches-output-file, or can generate a JS object with those frequencies if you run with --expose-ignition-statistics and call the extension function getIgnitionDispatchCounters().

args.name: v8_enable_builtins_frame_elision
args.comment: Enable frame elision for builtins.

args.name: v8_enable_builtins_profiling
args.comment: Runs mksnapshot with --turbo-profiling. After building in this configuration, any subsequent run of d8 will output information about usage of basic blocks in builtins.

args.name: v8_enable_builtins_profiling_verbose
args.comment: Runs mksnapshot with --turbo-profiling-verbose. After building in this configuration, any subsequent run of d8 will output information about usage of basic blocks in builtins, including the schedule and disassembly of all used builtins.

args.name: v8_log_builtins_block_count_input
args.comment: This build flag is used to input a builtin pgo file containing raw execution counts (as opposed to branch hints), which are embedded into the `--trace-turbo` .json file from `mksnapshot`.

args.name: v8_enable_builtins_block_position
args.comment: This build flag is used to enable block positioning in profile-guided optimization of builtins based on raw execution counts (as opposed to branch hints).

args.name: v8_enable_builtins_reordering
args.comment: This build flag is used to control whether reorder builtins according to the call graph with C3 algorithm based builtin PGO profiling.

args.name: v8_builtins_profiling_log_file
args.comment: Provides the given V8 log file as an input to mksnapshot, where it can be used for profile-guided optimization of builtins.  To do profile-guided optimizations of builtins: 1. Build with v8_enable_builtins_profiling = true 2. Run your chosen workload with the --turbo-profiling-output flag. For Chrome, the invocation might look like this: chrome --no-sandbox --disable-extensions --js-flags="--turbo-profiling-output=v8.builtins.pgo" "http://localhost/test-suite" 3. Run tools/builtins-pgo/get_hints.py to produce the branch hints, selecting min_count and threshold_ratio as you wish. 4. Optionally repeat steps 2-3 for additional workloads, and use tools/builtins-pgo/combine_hints.py to combine the hints produced in step 3 into a single file. 5. Build again with v8_builtins_profiling_log_file set to the file created in step 3 or 4.

args.name: v8_enable_builtins_gearbox
args.comment: Enable gearbox (instruction set extension) for builtins, the gearbox feature only supports x64 arch yet. Sets -DV8_BUILTINS_GEARBOX

args.name: v8_enable_test_features
args.comment: Enables various testing features.

args.name: v8_enable_short_builtin_calls
args.comment: Enable short builtins call instruction sequences by un-embedding builtins. Sets -DV8_SHORT_BUILTIN_CALLS

args.name: v8_enable_external_code_space
args.comment: Enable support for external code range relative to the pointer compression cage. Sets -DV8_EXTERNAL_CODE_SPACE

args.name: v8_postmortem_support
args.comment: With post mortem support enabled, metadata is embedded into libv8 that describes various parameters of the VM for use by debuggers. See tools/gen-postmortem-metadata.py for details.

args.name: v8_use_siphash
args.comment: Use Siphash as added protection against hash flooding attacks.

args.name: v8_no_inline
args.comment: Switches off inlining in V8.

args.name: v8_os_page_size
args.comment: Override OS page size when generating snapshot

args.name: v8_can_use_fpu_instructions
args.comment: Similar to vfp but on MIPS.

args.name: v8_use_mips_abi_hardfloat
args.comment: Similar to the ARM hard float ABI but on MIPS.

args.name: v8_typed_array_max_size_in_heap
args.comment: Controls the threshold for on-heap/off-heap Typed Arrays.

args.name: v8_check_header_includes
args.comment: Check that each header can be included in isolation (requires also setting the "check_v8_header_includes" gclient variable to run a specific hook).

args.name: v8_enable_lazy_source_positions
args.comment: Enable lazy source positions by default.

args.name: v8_disable_write_barriers
args.comment: Disable write barriers when GCs are non-incremental and heap has single generation.

args.name: v8_enable_unconditional_write_barriers
args.comment: Ensure that write barriers are always used. Useful for debugging purposes.

args.name: v8_enable_single_generation
args.comment: Redirect allocation in young generation so that there will be only one single generation.

args.name: v8_enable_regexp_interpreter_threaded_dispatch
args.comment: Use token threaded dispatch for the regular expression interpreter. Use switch-based dispatch if this is false

args.name: v8_verify_builtins_compatibility
args.comment: Enforce equality of builtins hashes from compatible architectures.

args.name: v8_verify_deterministic_mksnapshot
args.comment: Check mksnapshot determinism by running it multiple times.

args.name: v8_verify_torque_generation_invariance
args.comment: Enable additional targets necessary for verification of torque file generation

args.name: v8_annotate_torque_ir
args.comment: Generate comments describing the Torque intermediate representation.

args.name: target_os
args.comment: Enable snapshot compression (enabled by default for desktop) devices.

args.name: v8_control_flow_integrity
args.comment: Enable control-flow integrity features, such as pointer authentication for ARM64. Enable it by default for simulator builds and when native code supports it as well. On Mac, control-flow integrity does not work so we avoid enabling it when using the simulator.

args.name: v8_current_cpu
args.comment: Enable heap reservation of size 4GB. Only possible for 64bit archs.

args.name: cppgc_enable_verify_heap
args.comment: Enables additional heap verification phases and checks.

args.name: cppgc_allow_allocations_in_prefinalizers
args.comment: Enable allocations during prefinalizer invocations.

args.name: v8_enable_sandbox
args.comment: Enable the V8 sandbox. Sets -DV8_ENABLE_SANDBOX.

args.name: v8_enable_sandbox_hardware_support
args.comment: Enable experimental hardware support for the V8 sandbox if available. Sets -DV8_ENABLE_SANDBOX_HARDWARE_SUPPORT

args.name: v8_enable_memory_corruption_api
args.comment: Enable the memory corruption API. Useful for testing the sandbox. The memory corruption API is only exposed to JavaScript if sandbox testing mode is enabled at runtime, for example via --sandbox-fuzzing. WARNING This will enable builtins that (by design) cause memory corruption. Sets -DV8_ENABLE_MEMORY_CORRUPTION_API

args.name: v8_enable_precise_zone_stats
args.comment: Experimental feature for collecting per-class zone memory stats. Requires use_rtti = true

args.name: v8_monolithic_for_shared_library
args.comment: Set this if V8 monolithic static library is going to be linked into another shared library.

args.name: v8_enable_swiss_name_dictionary
args.comment: Experimental feature that uses SwissNameDictionary instead of NameDictionary as the backing store for all dictionary mode objects.

args.name: v8_generate_external_defines_header
args.comment: If enabled then macro definitions that are used in externally visible header files are placed in a separate header file v8-gn.h.

args.name: v8_dict_property_const_tracking
args.comment: Experimental feature for tracking constness of properties in non-global dictionaries. Enabling this also always keeps prototypes in dict mode, meaning that they are not switched to fast mode. Sets -DV8_DICT_PROPERTY_CONST_TRACKING

args.name: v8_enable_map_packing
args.comment: Enable map packing & unpacking (sets -DV8_MAP_PACKING).

args.name: v8_enable_javascript_promise_hooks
args.comment: Allow for JS promise hooks (instead of just C++).

args.name: v8_enable_continuation_preserved_embedder_data
args.comment: Allow embedder data to be saved on continuations. Used to support TaskAttribution and `scheduler.yield()`. The flag enables disabling the feature, to test this data's overhead.

args.name: v8_enable_allocation_folding
args.comment: Enable allocation folding globally (sets -DV8_ALLOCATION_FOLDING). When it's disabled, the --turbo-allocation-folding runtime flag will be ignored.

args.name: v8_enable_heap_snapshot_verify
args.comment: Enable runtime verification of heap snapshots produced for devtools.

args.name: v8_allocation_site_tracking
args.comment: Enable global allocation site tracking.

args.name: v8_scriptormodule_legacy_lifetime
args.comment: TODO(cbruni, v8:12302): Remove once API is migrated Enable legacy mode for ScriptOrModule's lifetime. By default it's a temporary object, if enabled it will be kept alive by the parent Script. This is only used by nodejs.

args.name: v8_enable_pointer_compression_8gb
args.comment: Enables pointer compression for 8GB heaps. Sets -DV8_COMPRESS_POINTERS_8GB.

args.name: v8_use_zlib
args.comment: Compile V8 using zlib as dependency. Sets -DV8_USE_ZLIB

args.name: v8_value_deserializer_hard_fail
args.comment: Make ValueDeserializer crash if the data to deserialize is invalid.

args.name: v8_jitless
args.comment: Enable jitless mode, including compile-time optimizations. Note that even when this is set to 'false', one can run V8 in jitless mode at runtime by passing the `--jitless` flag; but then you miss out on compile-time optimizations.

args.name: v8_enable_sparkplug
args.comment: Enable Sparkplug Sets -DV8_ENABLE_SPARKPLUG.

args.name: v8_enable_maglev_graph_printer
args.comment: Enable Maglev's graph printer. Sets -DV8_ENABLE_MAGLEV_GRAPH_PRINTER.

args.name: v8_enable_slow_tracing
args.comment: Enable slow tracing, e.g., tracing on every instruction or Turbofan node. Sets -DV8_ENABLE_SLOW_TRACING.

args.name: v8_enable_extensible_ro_snapshot
args.comment: Whether custom embedder snapshots may extend (= allocate new objects in) ReadOnlySpace.

args.name: v8_enable_black_allocated_pages
args.comment: Black allocate objects on separate pages.

args.name: v8_enable_sticky_mark_bits
args.comment: Use sticky mark-bits for separating object generations.

args.name: v8_enable_experimental_tsa_builtins
args.comment: Use the experimental TSA-based definition for some builtins.

args.name: v8_enable_experimental_tq_to_tsa
args.comment: Use the experimental TSA backend for Torque.

args.name: v8_enable_undefined_double
args.comment: Use the encoding of undefined in double values.

args.name: v8_depend_on_icu_data_file
args.comment: Remote builds require an explicit dependency on icudat, but this breaks locally building V8 with ICU support when the file isn't present, which some embedders rely on. This option controls the explicit dependency and allows the build to complete.

args.name: v8_lower_limits_mode
args.comment: Experimental testing mode where various limits are artificially set lower.

args.name: v8_enable_partition_alloc
args.comment: Enables the use of partition_alloc as the default allocator for standalone V8. This should be used for benchmarking and testing purposes to more closely mimic in-browser behavior.  Currently used in d8.

args.name: use_cppgc_clang_plugin
args.comment: V8 uses Chrome's blink-gc clang plugin for checking V8's internal cppgc usages. The plugin is not exhaustive but covers most common known pitfalls.

args.name: v8_function_arguments_caller_are_own_props
args.comment: Expose F.p.caller and .arguments as own properties.

args.name: v8_use_default_hasher_secret
args.comment: Use a hard-coded secret value when hashing.

args.name: v8_enable_seeded_array_index_hash
args.comment: Enable seeded array index hash.

args.name: v8_dumpling
args.comment: add instrumentation for Dumpling differential fuzzing

args.name: v8_riscv_enable_deprecated_riscv32
args.comment: Enable compilation of riscv32.

args.name: v8_logging_level
args.comment: Sets the level of logging detail in V8_Fatal. 0: No file and line and message only if args are provided (for official release builds). 1: Message but no file or line. 2: Everything (file, line, message).

args.name: v8_correctness_fuzzer
args.comment: Includes files needed for correctness fuzzing.

args.name: v8_multi_arch_build
args.comment: Adds additional compile target for building multiple architectures at once.

args.name: v8_has_valgrind
args.comment: Indicate if valgrind was fetched as a custom deps to make it available on swarming.

args.name: v8_gcmole
args.comment: Indicate if gcmole was fetched as a hook to make it available on swarming.

args.name: v8_optimized_debug
args.comment: Turns on compiler optimizations in V8 in Debug build.

args.name: v8_enable_backtrace
args.comment: Support for backtrace_symbols on linux.

args.name: v8_use_external_startup_data
args.comment: Use external files for startup data blobs: the JS builtins sources and the start snapshot.

args.name: v8_enable_builtins_optimization
args.comment: Includes profiles to optimize builtins.

args.name: v8_enable_debugging_features
args.comment: Turns on all V8 debug features. Enables running V8 in a pseudo debug mode within a release Chrome. This implies verification features to detect more bugs and additionally includes features that aid developers when debugging.

args.name: v8_enable_i18n_support
args.comment: Enable ECMAScript Internationalization API. Enabling this feature will add a dependency on the ICU library.

args.name: v8_enable_temporal_support
args.comment: Enable Temporal API. Enabling this feature will add a dependency on the temporal_rs library.  We currently only want it enabled on d8 and chromium builds (Functionality is still not accessible unless --harmony-temporal is enabled at runtime)  Furthermore, some architectures don't have Rust toolchains in Chromium

args.name: v8_static_library
args.comment: Use static libraries instead of source_sets.

args.name: v8_enable_cluster_build
args.comment: Enable cluster build. This build mode compiles multiple .cc files in one compilation unit. These are files where the C++ compiler spends most of its time processing the same long list of .h files, which means they can be compiled together in almost the same time that just one of them would have taken.

args.name: v8_monolithic
args.comment: Enable monolithic static library for embedders.

args.name: v8_expose_public_symbols
args.comment: Expose public symbols for native modules of Node.js and Electron. Default is false.

args.name: v8_expose_symbols
args.comment: Deprecated for v8_expose_public_symbols.

args.name: v8_use_perfetto
args.comment: Implement tracing using Perfetto (https://perfetto.dev).

args.name: v8_use_perfetto_json_export
args.comment: Use Perfetto JSON Export.

args.name: v8_use_perfetto_sdk
args.comment: Use Perfetto SDK headers.

args.name: v8_symbol_level
args.comment: Override global symbol level setting for v8.

args.name: v8_enable_wasm_gdb_remote_debugging
args.comment: Enable WebAssembly debugging via GDB-remote protocol.

args.name: v8_enable_lite_mode
args.comment: Lite mode disables a number of performance optimizations to reduce memory at the cost of performance. Sets -DV8_LITE_MODE.

args.name: v8_enable_drumbrake
args.comment: WebAssembly interpreter (DrumBrake)  build flag.

args.name: v8_enable_drumbrake_tracing
args.comment: Enable Wasm interpreter tracing.

args.name: v8_use_host_cpu_arm_features
args.comment: Use host CPU ARM features when compiling ARM/ARM64 snapshots (using mksnapshot). When true, use both CPU features from the target's CPU, and the host's CPU. Since mksnapshot's output runs on the target device, this is not desirable in general to take the CPU features of the host compiler. However, the default is true in order to preserve existing behavior, and ensure all necessary CPU features are available in certain scenarios (where target and host have the same CPU architecture but different CPUs).

args.name: v8_enable_turbofan
args.comment: Enable the Turbofan compiler. Sets -dV8_ENABLE_TURBOFAN.

args.name: v8_enable_maglev
args.comment: Enable the Maglev compiler. Sets -dV8_ENABLE_MAGLEV

args.name: v8_enable_webassembly
args.comment: Include support for WebAssembly. If disabled, the 'WebAssembly' global will not be available, and embedder APIs to generate WebAssembly modules will fail. Also, asm.js will not be translated to WebAssembly and will be executed as standard JavaScript instead. Sets -dV8_ENABLE_WEBASSEMBLY.

args.name: v8_enable_wasm_simd256_revec
args.comment: Enable 256-bit long vector re-vectorization pass in WASM compilation pipeline.

args.name: v8_enable_runtime_call_stats
args.comment: Enable runtime call stats.

args.name: v8_fuzzilli
args.comment: Add fuzzilli fuzzer support.

args.name: v8_force_optimize_speed
args.comment: Force optimizing with -O3 for increased inlining.

args.name: v8_enable_fuzztest
args.comment: Enable FuzzTest

args.name: v8_enable_direct_handle
args.comment: Use direct pointers in handles (v8::internal::Handle and v8::Local).

args.name: v8_enable_local_off_stack_check
args.comment: Check for off-stack allocated local handles.

args.name: cppgc_enable_api_checks
args.comment: Enables certain checks on API level functionality.

args.name: cppgc_enable_slow_api_checks
args.comment: Enable slow checks on API level functionality.

args.name: cppgc_enable_object_names
args.comment: Enable object names in cppgc for profiling purposes.

args.name: cppgc_enable_young_generation
args.comment: Enable young generation in cppgc.

args.name: cppgc_enable_slim_write_barrier
args.comment: Enables a slim write barrier that only performs a single check in the fast path and delegates all further checks to a slow path call. This is fast in a setting with few slow-path checks, i.e., with disabled young generation GC.

args.name: cppgc_enable_pointer_compression
args.comment: Enable pointer compression in cppgc. Note: this is automatically turned on by cppgc_enable_caged_heap on 64-bit platforms. To unconditionally disable pointer compression, set cppgc_enable_caged_heap = false.

args.name: cppgc_enable_larger_cage
args.comment: Enable support for larger cages, up to 16GB. iOS cannot mmap above 8GB, so use the smaller cage.

args.name: v8_advanced_bigint_algorithms
args.comment: Enable advanced BigInt algorithms, costing about 10-30 KB binary size depending on platform. Disabled on Android to save binary size.

args.name: v8_use_libm_trig_functions
args.comment: TODO: macros for determining endian type are clang specific.

args.name: v8_icu_path
args.comment: Location of icu.

args.name: v8_zlib_path
args.comment: Location of zlib.

args.name: v8_enable_pointer_compression
args.comment: Enable pointer compression (sets -dV8_COMPRESS_POINTERS).

args.name: v8_contiguous_compressed_ro_space_size_mb
args.comment: When `v8_enable_pointer_compression_shared_cage` RO space is placed into a contiguous area at the front of the cage. In case RO allocations fails this size needs to be adjusted.

args.name: v8_enable_cet_shadow_stack
args.comment: Change code emission and runtime features to be CET shadow-stack compliant (incomplete and experimental).

args.name: v8_enable_cet_ibt
args.comment: Emit CET IBT landing pad instructions in JIT generated code (experimental).

args.name: v8_enable_memory_sealing
args.comment: Use memory sealing to protect various global memory mappings for CFI (experimental). TODO(sroettger): enable by default once we have bot support for testing.

args.name: v8_enable_etw_stack_walking
args.comment: Sets -DV8_ENABLE_ETW_STACK_WALKING. Enables ETW Stack Walking

args.name: v8_target_is_simulator
args.comment: Specifies if the target build is a simulator build. By default it is set to true if the host and target do not match and we are not cross-compiling.

args.name: v8_wasm_random_fuzzers
args.comment: Some fuzzers depend on fuzzing functionality linked into the v8 library. For binary size reasons this functionality is not always available.

args.name: v8_enable_verification_features
args.comment: Enable more (costly) verification features that help to detect bugs.
